acceptance_targets: [CH13_CRAFT_OVERSTATEMENT_AFTER_ARRIVAL] After snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:179, the revised chapter must either introduce one new dramatic development that was not already legible from the solo swim (a measurable consequence, a relationship shift, or a decision), or stop translating the same understanding into abstract summary. The ending fails if it still paraphrases that Lena is slower, blunter, ordinary, and hers without adding new pressure.
[R008] Ch 13 does not re-explain Dara's flat affect as a general trait or re-label her stroke as the Bergen program's signature.
[R010] Ch 13's pool scene does not state the water temperature numerically or describe the changing-room-to-lane-to-dive entry sequence in more than one sentence.
[R011] Ch 13 contains no more than two references to the eighteen-year / since-age-four timeline.
[R026] Ch 13 contains no more than 10 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 121 prose lines).
[R036] Ch 13 has no more than 25 sentences (≤7% of ~365) beginning with 'She.'
[R048] Ch 13 contains no more than 6 instances of 'something.'

findings.acceptance_test: After snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:179, the revised chapter must either introduce one new dramatic development that was not already legible from the solo swim (a measurable consequence, a relationship shift, or a decision), or stop translating the same understanding into abstract summary. The ending fails if it still paraphrases that Lena is slower, blunter, ordinary, and hers without adding new pressure.
findings.problem: The chapter lands Lena's new ordinariness during the first solo swim, then spends the Dara exchange and closing paragraphs re-proving the same point in cleaner thematic language. Because those later beats add little new consequence, public pressure, or irreversible decision, the back half reads as certification of an already-legible state rather than a fresh turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the late movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:213 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241. Either make Dara's arrival create a new piece of pressure that was not already present by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:179, or compress the exchange so it functions as brief external witness. Then revise the close at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:243 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:247 so the chapter ends on a new concrete cost or on Lena's physical act of swimming, instead of restating that she is slower, blunter, ordinary, and hers after the pool scene has already shown it.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 does not re-explain Dara's flat affect as a general trait or re-label her stroke as the Bergen program's signature.
findings.evidence: Ch 13 line 2745: 'It came out as flat observation, which was the way Dara said most things.'; Ch 2 line 244: information 'delivered as information. Nothing in her face suggested that it was strange.'; Ch 13 line 2767: 'her stroke the Bergen program's signature pattern: metronomic, seamlessly paced.'; Ch 2 line 252: 'The Bergen program was different from Meridian's.'
findings.problem: Dara's flat affect and Bergen program affiliation are re-described in Ch 13 using nearly identical framing as Ch 2 establishment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Enter Dara through what Lena now sees differently post-patch rather than re-establishing the baseline. Cut the Bergen program label if the stroke description can stand alone.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13's pool scene does not state the water temperature numerically or describe the changing-room-to-lane-to-dive entry sequence in more than one sentence.
findings.evidence: Ch 13 line 2727: 'The water was the standard twenty-seven degrees...and the chlorine hit her in the usual way.'; Ch 13 lines 2729-2731: changing in locker room, walking to lane, diving in, underwater acoustics described from scratch; Ch 10 line 1778: 'twenty-seven degrees, a degree warmer than the air'; Ch 2 line 202+: first pool establishment.
findings.problem: Ch 13's pool scene re-inventories the space from scratch: water temperature (27 degrees, matching Ch 10), chlorine, entry ritual (changing, walking to lane, dive), and underwater acoustics (matching Ch 2). The pool was established in Ch 2 and the reader last saw it two chapters earlier in Ch 10.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the temperature callout. Compress the entry-ritual sequence to one sentence. The post-patch sensory contrast ('the water just sounded like water') is the valuable new content; foreground that and strip the re-inventory.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 contains no more than two references to the eighteen-year / since-age-four timeline.
findings.evidence: Ch 13 contains four 'eighteen years' references: line 2599, line 2653 ('eighteen years of calibration sessions'), line 2671 ('eighteen years of being calibrated'), line 2675 ('for eighteen years'). Manuscript-wide, Lena's four-year-old/eighteen-year baseline is proved ~10 times: Ch 2 lines 202, 204, 318; Ch 7 line 1273; Ch 10 lines 1910, 1992; Ch 13 lines 2599, 2653, 2671, 2675; Ch 17 line 3551; Ch 18 line 3806.
findings.problem: The fact that Lena has been enhanced since age four / for eighteen years is stated ~10 times across the manuscript, with a dense cluster of four instances in Ch 13 alone. While the Ch 13 patch-installation context provides new leverage, four separate 'eighteen years' callouts within a single chapter exceeds what the reader needs.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the strongest Ch 13 instance (line 2653, 'Your system's been learning how you swim since you were four') which provides structural consequence. Cut or vary the other three instances within Ch 13. Manuscript-wide, retain 4-5 total instances and cut the rest.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 contains no more than 10 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 121 prose lines).
findings.evidence: Ch 13: 46/121 prose lines contain em-dashes = 38.0%. Manuscript-wide: 654/1876 = 34.9% (threshold 10%, target <8%).
findings.problem: Ch 13 has em-dash density of 38.0%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 13 from 46 to ~10 (36 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 has no more than 25 sentences (≤7% of ~365) beginning with 'She.'
findings.problem: Ch 13 has 'She'-opener density of 17.3%, well above the 7% threshold. This creates a rhythmic monotony where consecutive sentences beginning with 'She' flatten the prose into a subject-verb march.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 13 from 63 to ~25 (38 rewrites). Vary sentence openings: lead with setting, sensory detail, dependent clauses, object-first constructions, or dialogue attributions.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 contains no more than 6 instances of 'something.'
findings.evidence: Ch 13: 'something' appears 14 times in 5105 words = 1 per 365 words. Manuscript-wide: 190 occurrences in 78,027 words = 1 per 411 words (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: 'Something' appears 14 times in Ch 13, a rate of 1 per 365 words. This vague pronoun substitutes for specific imagery. Manuscript-wide target: reduce from 190 to ~98 occurrences.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Ch 13 from 14 to ~6 (8 cuts). Replace each cut with the specific noun, sensation, or concept the 'something' stands in for. Keep 'something' only where vagueness is the point (character genuinely cannot name the experience).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
